stepAnswer: When A and B point in opposite directions $$(angle=180\degree)$$
stepTitle: What angle gives the smallest resultant?
stepBody: When is the magnitude of $$R=A+B$$ minimized?
answerLatex: When A and B point in opposite directions $$(angle=180\degree)$$
choices: When A and B point in the same direction $$(angle=0\degree)$$
When A and B are perpendicular $$(angle=90\degree)$$
When A and B point in opposite directions $$(angle=180\degree)$$
The magnitude is always the same
